description: Handles rendering the UI with the `rich` library.

classes.name: DisplayManager
classes.docstring:
Exclusively handles rendering the UI with the `rich` library, using data
    received from the provider.

    This class is responsible for pure UI rendering without any data fetching or business logic.

methods.name: __init__
methods.docstring:
Initializes the display manager with an injected data provider.

        Args:
            provider (DashboardDataProvider): The data provider for the dashboard.
            logger (StructuredLogger): The logger for recording operations.
            refresh_rate (int, optional): The refresh rate for the display in seconds. Defaults to 2.
            timezone_hours (int, optional): The timezone hours offset from UTC (Seoul = 9). Defaults to 9.

methods.name: _create_layout
methods.docstring:
Creates and returns the main layout structure for the dashboard.

        Returns:
            Layout: The main layout structure.

methods.name: start
methods.docstring: Starts the display update loop in a separate thread.

methods.name: stop
methods.docstring: Stops the display update loop and cleans up resources.

methods.name: _update_loop
methods.docstring: The main update loop that runs in a separate thread.

methods.name: update_display
methods.docstring: Updates the display with fresh data from the provider.

methods.name: _create_system_stats_panel
methods.docstring:
Creates a panel for system statistics.

        Args:
            stats_data (dict): A dictionary of system statistics.

        Returns:
            Panel: A `rich` Panel object.

methods.name: _create_gpu_panel
methods.docstring:
Creates a panel for GPU resources.

        Args:
            gpu_data (list): A list of GPU resource data.

        Returns:
            Panel: A `rich` Panel object.

methods.name: _create_cases_panel
methods.docstring:
Creates a panel for active cases.

        Args:
            cases_data (list): A list of active case data.

        Returns:
            Panel: A `rich` Panel object.
